serviceDescription.aws: AWS Network Firewall is a managed service that makes it easy to deploy essential network protections for all of your Amazon Virtual Private Clouds (VPCs). The service can be setup with just a few clicks and scales automatically with your network traffic, so you don't have to worry about deploying and managing any infrastructure.
serviceDescription.aws-cn: Amazon Network Firewall is a managed service that makes it easy to deploy essential network protections for all of your Amazon Virtual Private Clouds (VPCs). The service can be setup with just a few clicks and scales automatically with your network traffic, so you don't have to worry about deploying and managing any infrastructure.

description.aws: AWS Network Firewall is a managed service that makes it easy to deploy essential network protections for all of your Amazon Virtual Private Clouds (VPCs). The service can be setup with just a few clicks and scales automatically with your network traffic, so you don't have to worry about deploying and managing any infrastructure. To enable Network Firewall for your VPC, you perform steps in both Amazon VPC and in Network Firewall.
description.aws-cn: Amazon Network Firewall is a managed service that makes it easy to deploy essential network protections for all of your Amazon Virtual Private Clouds (VPCs). The service can be setup with just a few clicks and scales automatically with your network traffic, so you don't have to worry about deploying and managing any infrastructure. To enable Network Firewall for your VPC, you perform steps in both Amazon VPC and in Network Firewall.

title.aws: AWS Network Firewall Endpoint Hourly Charges
title.aws-cn: Amazon Network Firewall Endpoint Hourly Charges

helpText.aws: With AWS Network Firewall, you pay an hourly rate for each firewall endpoint. You also pay for the amount of traffic, billed by the gigabyte, processed by your firewall endpoint. Data processing charges apply for each Gigabyte processed through the firewall endpoint regardless of the traffic's source or destination. You also incur standard AWS data transfer charges for all data transferred via the AWS Network Firewall. Some configurations can incur additional costs, on top of the basic costs for using Network Firewall. For example, if you use a firewall endpoint in one Availability Zone to filter traffic from another zone, you can incur cross-zone traffic charges. If you enable logging, you incur additional charges according to factors such as the logging destination that you use and the amount of traffic that you choose to log. A VPC endpoint enables private connections between your VPC and supported AWS services. Your AWS Network Firewall endpoint must be deployed in a dedicated subnet within your Amazon VPC. AWS Network Firewall inspects all traffic that is routed to the endpoint, which is the mechanism for path insertion and filtering. See AWS Network Firewall documentation for details: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/network-firewall.html

If you enable logging, you incur additional charges according to factors such as the logging destination that you use and the amount of traffic that you choose to log. A VPC endpoint enables private connections between your VPC and supported Amazon services. Your Amazon Network Firewall endpoint must be deployed in a dedicated subnet within your Amazon VPC. Amazon Network Firewall inspects all traffic that is routed to the endpoint, which is the mechanism for path insertion and filtering. See Amazon Network Firewall documentation for details

content.aws: Tip: A common configuration is to have a firewall endpoint in each availability zone where you have VPC subnets that you want to protect. (Reference our service documentation at this link)
content.aws-cn: Tip: A common configuration is to have a firewall endpoint in each availability zone where you have VPC subnets that you want to protect. (Reference our service documentation)

Data processing charges apply for each Gigabyte processed through the firewall endpoint regardless of the traffic's source or destination. See Amazon Network Firewall pricing page for details:

content.aws: You also incur standard AWS data transfer charges for all data transferred via the AWS Network Firewall (i.e., out from EC2 to the Internet.) EC2 data transfer charges also apply if your NAT gateway and Network Firewall are in different availability zones. See AWS Network Firewall pricing page for details.

EC2 data transfer charges also apply if your NAT gateway and Network Firewall are in different availability zones. See Amazon Network Firewall pricing page for details.
